title: hymenolepis calva
intro: Hymenolepis calva, also known as calva, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues, including digestive problems and fever. The calva plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing swelling and fighting infections. Herbal preparations of Hymenolepis calva can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
